skill.name: launch-strategy
skill.description: Guides SaaS teams through product launches, feature announcements, Product Hunt campaigns, and post-launch momentum planning.
skill.summary: Plan phased SaaS launches across owned, rented, and borrowed channels.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review found marketing strategy text rather than executable code. The skill contains no scripts, network access, environment access, command execution, or prompt-injection language. The remaining risk is low and limited to normal marketplace reliance on unverified marketing advice.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is YAML description text about product launches and announcements. It does not define, call, or recommend any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text for skill routing. No code, cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, key handling, or executable instruction appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line introduces borrowed marketing channels and uses ordinary launch strategy language. It has no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section describes audience development tactics. There is no executable code or reference to encryption, hashing, ciphers, or credentials.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line refers to a decision matrix for marketing announcements. This is a business planning framework, not a cryptographic primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term appears in marketing guidance context. The section classifies major, medium, and minor updates and contains no code or security mechanism.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged checklist item says a roundup email should include the announcement. It does not involve hashing, encryption, or unsafe cryptographic choices.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a launch checklist item for email marketing. No algorithm, code path, or sensitive data processing is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged case study says a company sent a product sample to a YouTuber and received sales from a review. It is marketing outreach, not host or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes influencer marketing results. It contains no commands, system inventory, network scanning, filesystem probing, or data collection instructions.

content.value_statement: Product launches often lose momentum because teams treat launch day as a single event. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create phased launch plans with channel strategy and follow-up actions.
actual_capabilities: Builds launch plans across internal, alpha, beta, early access, and full launch phases.
Maps owned, rented, and borrowed channels to launch goals.
Plans Product Hunt preparation, launch-day engagement, and follow-up steps.
Creates launch checklists for pre-launch, launch day, and post-launch work.
Prioritizes announcements for major, medium, and minor product updates.
Suggests questions to clarify audience, timeline, channels, and launch readiness.
limitations: Does not execute campaigns or connect to marketing platforms.
Does not verify live Product Hunt rules, rankings, or platform changes.
Requires user-provided product, audience, timeline, and channel details.
Provides marketing guidance, not legal, financial, or compliance advice.

use_cases.title: Prepare a SaaS launch
use_cases.description: Create a phased plan from early testing through general availability, including channel choices and launch assets.

use_cases.title: Announce a major feature
use_cases.description: Decide how much promotion a feature deserves and plan email, in-app, blog, and social announcements.

use_cases.title: Run a Product Hunt campaign
use_cases.description: Organize pre-launch preparation, launch-day engagement, and post-launch conversion actions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic launch plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a launch for [product or feature]. My audience is [audience], my timeline is [date], and my current channels are [channels].

prompt_templates.title: Choose launch channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the owned, rented, and borrowed channel framework to recommend launch channels for [product]. Include what each channel should do before and after launch day.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Product Hunt plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Product Hunt launch plan for [product]. Include preparation, listing assets, launch-day engagement, and follow-up to convert visitors.

prompt_templates.title: Design an ongoing launch system
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an ongoing launch system for our product updates. We ship [cadence], serve [segments], and want rules for major, medium, and minor announcements.

output_examples.input: Plan a beta launch for a workflow automation tool with a small waitlist.
output: Phase the launch from invited testers to controlled early access.
Use email as the owned channel, LinkedIn as the rented channel, and partner webinars as the borrowed channel.
Track activation feedback before expanding access.

output_examples.input: Help us decide how to announce a new integration.
output: Treat the integration as a medium update unless it changes a core workflow.
Send targeted email to affected segments and add an in-app banner.
Reserve full campaign effort for launches with broad product impact.

best_practices: Provide audience size, channel performance, launch date, and product maturity before asking for a plan.
Use owned channels to capture attention from rented and borrowed platforms.
Plan post-launch education and follow-up before the announcement goes live.
anti_patterns: Treating launch day as the entire launch strategy.
Relying only on social media without an owned audience path.
Promoting every small update with the same intensity as a major launch.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full launch timeline?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize internal, alpha, beta, early access, full launch, and post-launch actions.

faq.question: Does it support Product Hunt launches?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes preparation, listing, engagement, and follow-up guidance for Product Hunt campaigns.

faq.question: Can it write launch copy?
faq.answer: It can help plan launch messages and assets, but dedicated copywriting skills may provide deeper copy variants.

faq.question: Does it connect to email or social tools?
faq.answer: No. It provides strategy and checklists, but it does not access external platforms or publish content.

faq.question: What inputs make the output better?
faq.answer: Share the product type, target audience, launch goal, timeline, current channels, and past launch results.

faq.question: Is this only for new products?
faq.answer: No. It also supports feature announcements, product updates, early access programs, and ongoing launch systems.
